changes_made: Renamed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for consistency with resource naming conventions
review_notes: The functions generally map well to the FedRAMP CM-6 control, focusing on configuration settings in Kubernetes. The function names are mostly accurate and descriptive, but the audit logging function needed renaming for consistency with the specified resource naming conventions.
naming_improvements: Ensure resource names are consistent with the specified pattern, such as using 'apiserver' for audit logging related functions.
title: Configuration Settings
step1_reasoning: The CM-6 control focuses on configuration settings, which in Kubernetes can be mapped to ensuring audit logging is enabled to track configuration changes, ensuring etcd and secrets are encrypted to protect configuration data, and enforcing least privilege to control who can change configurations. These align with the FedRAMP control's intent to secure configuration settings and ensure they are properly monitored and protected.
